{{Quote|He's been obsessed ever since we split up on Farstine. For an arms dealer, he's kind of sweet; makes me feel wanted.|Kaliyo Djannis recalls splitting up with her former lover, Yjal|<ref name="Kaliyo">{{TORcite|type=conversation|Kaliyo Djannis|Kaliyo and Yjal}}</ref>}}
'''Farstine''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] within the [[Farstine system/Legends|Farstine system]], situated in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Ryndellian sector/Legends|Ryndellian sector]]. The planet was located on two major [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]]: the [[Five Veils Route]], which connected the Mid Rim to the [[Svivreni sector/Legends|Svivreni]] and [[Skine sector/Legends|Skine sectors]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], and the [[Triellus Trade Route/Legends|Triellus Trade Route]], which spanned a large section of the Outer Rim within [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. Farstine possessed a [[Methane/Legends|methane]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], requiring [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]]-breathing [[Species/Legends|species]] to use [[Breath mask/Legends|breath masks]] for survival on the surface. Relatively untouched by [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] events, a [[Battle of Farstine|battle]] was fought at Farstine in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] between [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|holdout]] forces commanded by [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Customs Vizier]] [[Marath Vooro]], and an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] headed by the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] [[Octavian Grant]]. The planet was known to be one of the [[Spice/Legends|spice]] trade's major transshipment hubs, and was under Imperial [[Occupation of Farstine|occupation]] at some point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Description==
Farstine was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="RPG" /> located in the [[Ryndellian sector/Legends|Ryndellian sector]]<ref name="TEA-OC" /> on the edge of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]].<ref name="AtlasMap36">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''—Client States and Miscellaneous Regions map</ref> Situated within the [[Farstine system/Legends|Farstine system]],<ref name="TEA-OC" /> Farstine was the starting point for the [[Five Veils Route]], a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade route]] that directly connected the world to [[Trigalis/Legends|Trigalis]]. The Five Veils Route,<ref name="TEA" /> also known as the Five Veils' Tour trade route,<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref> boasted two separate endpoints,<ref name="Fry">{{JCFCite|url=posts/50723299|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2013-05-16|archivedate=20150930074204}}</ref> connecting Farstine to the planets [[Skynara]] in the [[Skine sector/Legends|Skine sector]]<ref name="GG9" /> and [[Svivren]]<ref name="TEA" /> in the [[Svivreni sector/Legends|Svivreni sector]], both in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA-OC" /> Farstine was also located between the planets [[Zhar/Legends|Zhar]] and [[Ryndellia/Legends|Ryndellia]] on the [[Triellus Trade Route/Legends|Triellus Trade Route]],<ref name="TEA" /> a hyperlane that linked a swath of the Outer Rim contained within [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]], skirting [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] before turning into the Mid Rim<ref name="AtlasMap36" /> and terminating at [[Enarc/Legends|Enarc]].<ref name="TEA" /> Farstine had a [[Methane/Legends|methane]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]]-breathing [[Species/Legends|species]] required [[Breath mask/Legends|breath masks]] for survival.<ref name="RPG" /> By [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Farstine had a population numbering between 500 million to one billion inhabitants.<ref name="AtlasMap17" />

==History==
Farstine was discovered and settled sometime prior to the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]],<ref name="Kaliyo" /> an uneasy eleven-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] period of peace between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]] following the signing of the [[Treaty of Coruscant]], which ended the [[Great Galactic War]] in [[3653 BBY]].<ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref> The [[Rattataki/Legends|Rattataki]] [[Scoundrel/Legends|criminal]] [[Kaliyo Djannis]] visited the planet with her lover, the arms dealer [[Yjal]], before the war. They broke off their relationship while on the planet.<ref name="Kaliyo" /> Over two thousand [[Time/Legends|years]] later, during the [[New Sith Wars]]—which pitted the Republic against the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]<ref name="TEA" /> between [[1004 BBY]] and [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]—Farstine was located in disputed territory.<ref name="AtlasMap133">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''—Fall of the Republic and the New Sith Wars map</ref>

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] that raged from [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], fought between the Republic and the breakaway separatists of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]],<ref name="TEA" /> Farstine fell within Republic-controlled [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="AtlasMap151">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''—The Clone Wars map</ref> In 19 BBY, following the [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|end]] of the Clone Wars, the victorious Republic was [[Declaration of a New Order|transformed]] into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Customs Vizier]] [[Marath Vooro]] refused to recognize the legitimacy of the treaty that Acting [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Sentepeth Findos]] approved, which effectively dissolved the Trade Federation—a major contributor to the Confederacy of Independent Systems—by signing over all their assets to the Empire. Vooro retained command of [[Trade Federation fleet/Legends|Trade Federation Defense Fleet]] [[Starship/Legends|ships]] and [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] at Enarc, which lay on the Triellus Trade Route relatively close to Farstine. Seeking to bring various [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdouts]]—including Vooro—to heel, the Empire launched a series of campaigns to [[Reconquest of the Rim|reconquer]] the Outer Rim Territories. A [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] commanded by the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] [[Octavian Grant]] [[Space warfare/Legends|faced]] Vooro's forces at Farstine and [[Battle of Farstine|defeated]] them, forcing Vooro to retreat down the Five Veils Route. Grant subsequently [[Battle of the Hook Nebula|captured]] Vooro on the fringes of the [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]].<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Farstine was well-known as a major transshipment point for the [[Spice/Legends|spice]] trade, with consignments of the narcotic traveling along both the Triellus Trade Route and the Five Veils Route upon which Farstine was located.<ref name="AtlasMap182">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''—The Spice Trade map</ref> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], which was fought between the Empire and the insurgent [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], Imperial forces [[Occupation of Farstine|occupied]] Farstine. While the Empire was taking control of the planet, a [[Unidentified bounty hunter (Farstine)|bounty hunter]] encountered a [[Unidentified child (Farstine)|child]] who had been orphaned when his [[Parent/Legends|parents]] were [[Death/Legends|killed]] by Imperial [[Soldier/Legends|troops]]. The homeless, penniless [[Youngling/Legends|child]] had only fifteen [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] of oxygen remaining; taking pity on the boy, the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]—who privately admitted that the orphan stirred memories of a [[Unidentified bounty hunter's brother|younger brother]] who had died at an early age—took charge of the child.<ref name="RPG" />

The luxury [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] ''[[Veil of Skynara]]'' regularly ran cruises for tourists from Farstine to Skynara in the Skine [[Sector/Legends|sector]] during the Galactic Civil War.<ref name="GG9" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the Imperial [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]] used Farstine as a transit point en route from the [[Qeimet system/Legends|Qeimet system]] to the [[Eriadu system/Legends|Eriadu system]].<ref name="AtlasMap187">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''—The Empire Strikes Back map</ref> The [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] also employed the planet as a navigational stopover<ref name="AtlasMap191">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''—Return of the Jedi map</ref> as he traveled from the [[Vergesso Asteroids/Legends|Vergesso Asteroids]] in the [[Lybeya system/Legends|Lybeya system]] to the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] with the [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]-encased [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] in the hold of his starship, [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']].<ref name="TEA" /> In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], [[Thrawn's confederation|Imperial forces]] allied with [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] passed by Farstine on route to Svivren, where they laid [[Siege of Svivren|siege]] to the planet on [[Great ReSynchronization|44:7:24]] as part of Thrawn's [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government and successor state to the Alliance.<ref name="AtlasMap200">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''—Thrawn's Campaigns map</ref> By [[137 ABY]], Farstine fell within the space claimed by [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Empire]].<ref name="AtlasMap226">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''—The New Empire map</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Farstine was first mentioned in [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']], published by [[West End Games]] in [[1987]].<ref name="RPG" /> It was later mentioned in [[1993]]'s ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', also published by West End Games.<ref name="GG9" /> Farstine received an entry in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|257|Farstine}}</ref> and was mentioned in dialogue by the character Kaliyo Djannis in [[2011]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', an [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|MMORPG]] by [[BioWare]].<ref name="TOR" />

''Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim'' establishes that Farstine is the head of the Five Veils' Tour trade route, which linked Farstine to Skynara in the Skine sector.<ref name="GG9" /> In [[2009]]'s ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]], no mention was made of the Five Veils' Tour trade route, but a Five Veils Route was included, linking Farstine to Svivren in the Svivreni sector.<ref name="TEA" /> In a thread on the [[Jedi Council Forums]] on [[TheForce.net|TheForce.Net]], Fry clarified that the Five Veils' Tour trade route and the Five Veils Route were the same hyperlane and that the differing destinations were a result of tourism and shipping companies setting different endpoints.<ref name="Fry" />

Farstine's entry in ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' established that it was located in the Skine sector.<ref name="CSWE" /> While Skynara, the end point of the Five Veils Route, had been established to be located in the Skine sector in ''Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim'', Farstine had not.<ref name="GG9" /> The [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to ''The Essential Atlas'' established that Farstine was actually located in the Ryndellian sector.<ref name="TEA-OC" />

In ''The Essential Atlas'', a map entitled "Galactic Explorations" on page 120 charts when regions of the galaxy were first discovered and explored. Farstine is located within an area stated to have been explored between [[3000 BBY]] and 1,000 BBY.<ref name="TEA" /> However, as Farstine is mentioned in dialogue in ''Star Wars: The Old Republic'',<ref name="TOR" /> set between [[3643 BBY]] and [[3640 BBY]],<ref name="TOR dates">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' is set between [[3643 BBY]] and [[3640 BBY]] per the reasoning [[Template:TOR dates|here]].</ref> the planet must have been discovered prior to that date.<ref name="TOR" />
